reforms.description: The 1996 Budget reallocates up to C$ 260 million over three years towards technology and innovation, enhancing Canada's competitive edge. Additionally, the Technology Partnerships Canada (TPC) programme was introduced to provide reimbursable financing to firms for close-to-market development of key technologies, while the Canadian Technology Network (CTN) was launched to facilitate access for firms to business expertise and information on technology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding and support programs are designed to enhance innovation and competitiveness, which are expected to raise productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were enacted as part of the 1996 Budget and have been operational since then.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant funding reallocations and the introduction of new programs aimed at enhancing innovation, but they do not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The Canadian Technology Network (CTN), a grouping of industry associations, research organisations, government institutes, and universities, was launched in 1996 to provide firms with quick and easy access to business expertise, advice and information on technology.

reforms.description: The Internal Agreement on Trade includes a chapter aimed at improving inter-provincial labor mobility by eliminating barriers that restrict workers' access to employment opportunities across provinces. Additionally, a joint project between the Council of Ministers of Education Canada and Statistics Canada has been initiated to develop better measures of the performance of education systems, enhancing research and dissemination of information regarding best practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving labor mobility and education performance is expected to enhance the skills and capabilities of the workforce, contributing to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives have been operational since their introduction in 1996.
reforms.importance_rationale: These initiatives are significant in improving labor mobility and education performance but do not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The Agreement has a specific chapter dealing with labour mobility which aims to eliminate existing barriers that prevent a worker qualified for an occupation in one part of Canada having access to employment opportunities in that occupation elsewhere in the country.

reforms.description: The Technology Partnerships Programme, a joint initiative of three Research Councils, was established to create partnerships among universities and SMEs for research development and commercialization.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between universities and SMEs, the programme aims to enhance innovation and commercialization efforts, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been announced but details on implementation are not provided.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of this programme represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration and innovation but does not constitute a major overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The Technology Partnerships Programme, a joint initiative of three Research Councils, aims to create partnerships among universities and SMEs for research development and commercialisation.
